today_summary: GCM26 spot at $4,069.80, up $64.0 (+1.6%) off Tuesday's $4,005.8 low. ATM IV jumped to 23.94% (+3.47pp day/day), pricing a $61.4 (1σ) daily move and bracketing today's range at roughly $4,008–$4,131. Max pain sits at $4,750, +$680 (+16.7%) above spot and too distant to act as a near-term pin; call OI (138,112) still dwarfs put OI (77,378) for a 0.56 P/C ratio, though volume today was far more balanced (6,354 calls vs 5,856 puts). Net GEX within ±$200 of spot is -$107.5 — dealers remain short gamma and will amplify, not dampen, the next directional move.
five_day_thesis: Spot round-tripped over the past five sessions — down $151.5 to a $4,005.8 low on 7/14 before bouncing $64.0 to $4,069.80 today, for a net 5-day change of -$87.5 (-2.1%) from $4,157.3. The put wall ($4,000) and the scraper's static call print ($5,370, legacy OI +31% above spot) haven't moved in five sessions, but today's windowed OI shows real resistance sitting much closer in, at $4,700/$4,400/$4,800. Dealer gamma flipped negative on 7/9 and has stayed negative for four of the last five sessions (gex window: +28.2 → -51.3 → -14.2 → -102.5 → -30.9), deepening sharply into the 7/14 low before partially unwinding as spot bounced — the gamma flip strike has held in a tight $3,225–$3,330 band all week, always far below spot, meaning the entire tradable range has been structurally negative gamma. ATM IV expanded 2.33pp over the window (21.61% → 23.94%), with +3.47pp of that move landing in the single session between 7/14 and 7/15 — vol is expanding into the bounce rather than collapsing, a sign dealers are still pricing tail risk even as price stabilizes. Max pain drifted down modestly ($4,780 → $4,750, -$30) and has now held flat for three straight sessions, but at +16.7% above spot it remains a background gravity well, not a tradeable magnet.
what_changed: Spot round-tripped: down to a $4,005.8 low on 7/14, then +$64.0 (+1.6%) to $4,069.80 today, net -$87.5 (-2.1%) over 5 sessions.
ATM IV expanded 2.33pp over the window (21.61% → 23.94%), with +3.47pp of that move landing in the last session alone.
Dealer gamma deepened to -$102.5 (window) at the 7/14 low before partially unwinding to -$30.9 today — negative for 4 of the last 5 sessions.
Max pain drifted $4,780 → $4,750 (-$30) and has now held flat for three consecutive sessions.
P/C OI ratio held essentially flat (0.57 → 0.56) — the call-heavy 138,112-vs-77,378 OI structure is entrenched, not reactive to price.
Put wall ($4,000) and legacy call print ($5,370) unchanged all 5 sessions; real resistance sits closer in, at $4,700/$4,400/$4,800 today.
prediction: Bias is cautiously constructive above $4,000 — spot has already bounced $64 off that support and gamma has begun unwinding from its -$102.5 trough toward -$30.9, leaving room for a continued grind toward $4,131 (1σ upper band) and then $4,400, the nearest real call-OI wall. Below $4,000, the negative-gamma regime reactivates hard, as it did into the 7/14 low, opening a fast move toward $3,600 then $3,500. Invalidation: a daily close back below $4,000 flips the setup bearish and stalls the bounce thesis.

day_trade_cta.bias_note: Spot bounced $64.0 off the $4,000 put wall and gamma is unwinding from its -$102.5 trough (-$107.5 near spot on the tighter ±$200 window) — the setup favors continuation above $4,000, not a fresh breakdown.
day_trade_cta.entry_note: Buy dips toward the $4,000 put wall with confirmation; avoid chasing the bounce directly into the $4,700–$4,800 call OI cluster.
day_trade_cta.pivot_note: Sustained trade above → target $4,131 (1σ upper band) then $4,400. Sustained trade below $4,000 → negative-gamma cascade toward $3,600 then $3,500.
day_trade_cta.stop_note: A clean break below the $4,000 put wall invalidates the long and reactivates the negative-gamma cascade seen into the 7/14 low.
day_trade_cta.target_note: T1 is the 1σ upper band — take 50% off. T2 is the nearest real call-OI wall (4,521 contracts) — exit remaining.
day_trade_cta.risk_note: Net GEX -$107.5 near spot (±$200 window) with the gamma flip strike near $3,260 — dealers remain short gamma across the entire tradable range. Expect swings beyond the $61.4 (1σ) implied daily range. Reduce size 30-50%.
